water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing conditions on the Chattahoochee River are very good with water temperatures ideal for trout. Recent stocking supports active feeding on eggs, worms, and attractor patterns.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day1 morning because cooler temperatures and high humidity favor trout activity. Calm wind and moderate sky cover create ideal conditions for using nymphs, midges, and soft hackles in riffles and runs.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS water temperature is 57°F with flow at 1510 cfs and a gage height of 2.93 ft. Turbidity is moderate at 21 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 22%, pressure is 29.99 inHg with an unknown trend, wind is from the southwest at 4.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and light rain chance is low with 0.02 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Expect trout to position in riffles, runs, seams, and near structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover in bright conditions. Presentations should focus on clean drifts using smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell attractors. Use nymphs, midges, soft hackles, and opportunistic streamers for best results.
